rulings.Case Title: CHL INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHITO KUMAR SAHA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Complainants Vikrant Punj And American Indian Cuisine, Inc.'S First Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion Calendar for Friday, February 10, 2023, line 1. Cross-Complaint's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings as to Answer to Cross-Complaint is GRANTED with leave to amend. Each answering defendant shall either file a separate answer or verify a joint answer. Additionally, each affirmative defense shall allege facts in support of each element of the asserted affirmative defense. Cross-Defendants shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file amended answer(s). Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SANG AU SU, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL VS. NAM MUI AU, AN INDIVIDUAL ALSO KNOWN AS NAM MUOI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion Calendar for Friday, February 10, 2023, line 2. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. The complaint as alleged is uncertain. Multiple allegations are made regarding property transfers obtained by fraud and manipulation from Mui-Tai Vong, as trustee and as an individual starting with Grant Deed attached as Exhibit C to the complaint (Complaint, paragraph 16-18). However, in opposition Plaintiffs assert that they desire to quiet title based on the same Grant Deed attached as Exhibit C (which was purportedly obtained by fraud and manipulation) (See Opposition 5:24-26). Plaintiffs also assert that the estate of Mui-Tai Vong is not a necessary party to this action even though the operative Deed according to the Plaintiffs is the one vesting title in Mui-Tai Vong, an individual. Plaintiff further asserts that the statute of limitations does not bar this action because one of the Plaintiffs, signatory to the 2010 Agreement, doesn't read or write English. Plaintiffs fail to explain the relevancy of this assertion as it pertains to the statute of limitations based on the governing authorities re: contracts and torts. Plaintiff further claims that the probate proceeding has been resolved, however the Probate matter is still pending. Therefore, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly allege the basis for quiet title cause of action, the date as of which title is being quieted, what interest and in whom is being quieted. Furthermore, Plaintiff to allege facts addressing the statute of limitations. Additionally, this matter shall be stayed until the final resolution of the pending probate case and the First Amended Complaint shall be filed and served within 30 days of the final resolution of the Probate matter. The Court does not address any other arguments raised in the demurrer at this time pending the clarifications in the amended complaint. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: RAKESH PATEL VS. JEANETTE GUITERREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion Calendar for Friday, February 10, 2023, line 3. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Discovery is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Defendant to pay sanctions in the amount of $720 within 5 days of notice of entry of order.
